title: CSV to JSON CLI Tool
summary: A Python command-line interface (CLI) tool that validates and converts CSV files into JSON format, with robust error handling and user-friendly output.

user_stories.title: User can run the CLI tool with a CSV file
user_stories.description: The user should be able to execute the tool from the command line by providing a CSV file as input.
acceptance_criteria: The tool accepts a CSV file path as a command-line argument
The tool displays a usage message if no arguments are provided
The tool exits gracefully if the file does not exist

user_stories.title: User can validate the CSV file
user_stories.description: The tool should validate the CSV file structure before conversion.
acceptance_criteria: The tool checks for valid CSV syntax
The tool reports errors if the CSV is malformed or missing required fields
The tool provides detailed error messages for invalid data

user_stories.title: User can convert a valid CSV to JSON
user_stories.description: The tool should convert a valid CSV file into a JSON format.
acceptance_criteria: The tool generates a JSON file with the same name as the CSV file but with a .json extension
The JSON structure matches the CSV headers and rows
The tool outputs the JSON content to stdout if requested

user_stories.title: User can handle errors during conversion
user_stories.description: The tool should handle errors gracefully and provide informative feedback.
acceptance_criteria: The tool logs errors to the console
The tool does not crash on invalid data
The tool provides an option to continue processing after an error

user_stories.title: User can specify output format
user_stories.description: The user should have the ability to customize the JSON output format.
acceptance_criteria: The tool allows specifying whether to output as a list of objects or a single object
The tool supports custom field mappings if needed

non_functional_requirements.requirement: The tool should process large CSV files efficiently without excessive memory usage
The tool must provide clear and actionable error messages
The tool should have a simple and intuitive command-line interface
The tool should not corrupt the original CSV file during processing
The tool should work across different operating systems

constraints: The tool must be implemented in Python and should not require external libraries beyond standard Python packages.
assumptions: The CSV files will follow standard formats, and the user has basic knowledge of command-line operations.
out_of_scope: Support for non-standard CSV formats, such as those with embedded commas or special characters, is not included unless explicitly requested.
